title: cytisus tridentatus var. cantabricus
intro: Cytisus tridentatus var. cantabricus, also known as Cantabrica broom, is a variety of the Cytisus tridentatus plant, a species of broom native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a shrub with yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Cantabrica broom include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
